# Implementation Plan: Region Screen Container Marking

**Branch**: `002-region-screen-container` | **Date**: 2026-07-09 | **Spec**: [spec.md](spec.md)

**Input**: Feature specification from `specs/002-region-screen-container/spec.md`

**Note**: This plan is generated by the `/speckit-plan` command.

## Summary

This feature implements the capability to designate a single Scene Region on the canvas as the recipient/container for a shared screen feed. We will extend the core Foundry VTT v14 `RegionConfig` sheet via the `renderRegionConfig` hook to inject a checkbox option under the "Appearance" tab. To prevent multiple containers in a single scene, we will scan other region documents on the scene and disable the control if another container is already defined, displaying an informative conflict message. The state will be stored persistently and synchronized across all clients using a namespaced document flag: `flags.screen-share.isScreenContainer`.

## Technical Context

**Language/Version**: ES6 JavaScript (Foundry VTT v14 Client API).

**Primary Dependencies**: Foundry VTT v14 core APIs (Document flags, `RegionDocument`, and `RegionConfig` sheets).

**Storage**: Persistent world database via standard Foundry Document Flags. Stored at `flags.screen-share.isScreenContainer` (boolean).

**Testing**: Manual validation in local Foundry VTT v14 environment following the scenarios in [quickstart.md](quickstart.md).

**Target Platform**: Modern web browsers supported by Foundry VTT v14 (Chrome, Firefox, Safari, Edge).

**Project Type**: Foundry VTT Client Module.

**Performance Goals**: Instant UI rendering (under 5ms injection overhead), minimal memory footprint.

**Constraints**:
- Only one region per scene may be marked.
- UI control must be disabled with a descriptive note if another region in the scene has the container flag set.
- Only GMs can access the `RegionConfig` dialog and modify region flags.

## Constitution Check

*GATE: Must pass before Phase 0 research. Re-check after Phase 1 design.*

- **I. Decoupled Transmission Strategy**: Passed. This feature only handles setting the region container flag and configuration UI. It does not introduce or depend on any media streaming/WebRTC technology.
- **II. Explicit WebGL & Network Lifecycle Management**: Passed. No canvas rendering, WebGL textures, or WebRTC connections are managed in this feature.
- **III. Foundry VTT v14 API Standard Compliance**: Passed. We strictly use v14 Scene Regions and standard `RegionDocument` flags. We hook into `RegionConfig` sheet rendering instead of using legacy hacks.
- **IV. Dual-Role Signaling & Connection Architecture**: Passed. The UI control is embedded within `RegionConfig` which is GM-only, and any programmatic flag update is naturally gated by Foundry VTT client/server role validation.
- **V. Masked PixiJS Canvas Rendering**: Passed. No canvas rendering changes are made in this step.
